Dr. Nazrul Islam.longBio: Dr. Md. Nazrul Islam is a distinguished Professor at the Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU). With over two decades of research and teaching experience, he has made significant contributions to digital image encryption, cryptographic security protocols, pattern recognition, and speech technology. Under the Agri-VoiceLink project, he defines the high-level technical architecture, leading the design of bilingual speech transcription systems and supervising secure deep-learning edge diagnostics models.
responsibilities: Define the high-level technical architecture and deep learning methodologies for Agri-VoiceLink.
Supervise the fine-tuning of multi-lingual acoustic and speech-to-text models for rural Bengal.
Co-ordinate with regional agricultural centers and public extension offices for field diagnostics validation.

Nargis Akter.longBio: Nargis Akter is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU). She possesses deep expertise in data science, intelligent systems, and computational modeling. In the Agri-VoiceLink project, she serves as a Co-Investigator, contributing to linguistic corpus curation, acoustic mapping of local dialects, and localized model diagnostics validation.
responsibilities: Collaborate on the development of voice-first agricultural dialect mapping algorithms.
Support standard and colloquial corpus verification and text transcript processing.
Lead field diagnostic validation and usability studies with local farming communities in northern regions.

Md. Nafis Fuad.longBio: Md. Nafis Fuad is an active researcher and field assistant at the Department of ICT, Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU). He specializes in coordinating physical survey activities, engaging with regional rural farming communities, collecting and verifying dialect-rich vocal annotations, and establishing crop-growth dataset metadata repositories.
responsibilities: Coordinate administrative and logistical tasks for on-ground agricultural field surveys.
Collect, audit, and annotate dialect vocal samples directly from agricultural sites in Tangail.
Assist in training data collation and labeling of disease-stricken crop leaf images.

Nadim Chowdhury.longBio: Nadim Chowdhury is a highly motivated field coordinator and data collector for the Agri-VoiceLink project under the Department of ICT, MBSTU. His research interests focus on Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) in agricultural tech, community outreach, and the design of localized speech-to-intent applications to increase digital literacy among marginalized rural farmers.
responsibilities: Organize community outreach workshops and explain the voice-first application benefits directly to farmers.
Record dialect-specific speech variations under various background noise settings to train robust model encoders.
Compile agricultural taxonomy terms and disease labels with expert help.
